description: Hubble Ready CLI Testing Framework - Enable automated firmware testing with non-interactive commands, granular GATT operations, and validation suites

userStories.title: Add --address parameter to ready scan
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to filter scan results to a specific device address so that I can verify a specific device is advertising without interactive selection. Add --address parameter to existing ready scan command in cli.py (line 769). Filter devices list to exact MAC address match (case-insensitive). Return empty array if no match. Maintain backward compatibility when --address omitted.
acceptanceCriteria: Command executes: hubblenetwork ready scan --address AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF --format json
Returns empty array if device not found (exit code 0)
Returns single-element array if device found
Case-insensitive address matching works
Existing behavior unchanged when --address omitted
Tabular output shows only filtered device when --address provided
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add --address parameter to ready info
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want to connect directly to a device by address so that I can quickly inspect characteristics without interactive selection. Modify ready info command in cli.py (line 885) to accept --address parameter. When provided, skip scan and connect directly. On connection failure, return JSON error with exit code 2.
acceptanceCriteria: Command executes: hubblenetwork ready info --address AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF --format json
Connects directly without scanning when --address provided
Returns error with exit code 2 if connection fails
Existing scan+selection behavior unchanged when --address omitted
JSON output includes all characteristics on success
Tabular output works with --address parameter
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document --timeout parameter consistency
userStories.description: As a QA engineer, I want to control connection timeouts for all ready commands so that I can optimize test suite execution time. Document that all existing ready commands (scan, info, provision) support --timeout parameter with default values. Ensure timeout errors return exit code 2 consistently. This story is documentation-only since existing commands already have timeout support.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify all existing ready commands support --timeout parameter
Confirm default timeout values in code comments
Verify timeout errors return exit code 2 consistently
JSON error output includes timeout information
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add ATT error codes and BleError exception to errors.py
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want ATT error code constants and mappings defined so that I can properly handle and report GATT operation failures. Add BleError exception class with att_error_code attribute. Define 6 ATT error code constants (0x0D, 0x0F, 0x84-0x87). Add ATT_ERROR_NAMES dictionary for human-readable names. Implement extract_att_error_code() function to parse BleakError messages. Add BleError.to_dict() method for JSON serialization.
acceptanceCriteria: BleError exception class defined with att_error_code attribute
All 6 ATT error code constants defined
ATT_ERROR_NAMES dictionary maps all codes to names
extract_att_error_code() function parses BleakError messages
All new exports added to __all__
BleError.to_dict() returns JSON-serializable format
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update JSON output structure to specification format
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want all ready commands to output JSON in a consistent structure so that I can reliably parse test results. Create helper functions _format_ready_json_success() and _format_ready_json_error() in cli.py. Standard structure includes: success (bool), command (str), device (dict with address and name), result/error (dict), duration_ms (int). Update existing ready scan and ready info commands to use new format.
acceptanceCriteria: Helper functions _format_ready_json_success and _format_ready_json_error implemented
All top-level fields present: success, command, device, result/error, duration_ms
Error objects include code, name, message fields
BleError exceptions format correctly with ATT error codes
Device object includes address (required) and name (optional)
Duration tracking implemented using time.monotonic()
JSON is valid and parseable
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add WriteResult, TestResult, ValidationResult dataclasses
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want typed dataclasses for write results and test results so that the codebase maintains type safety. Add three dataclasses to ready.py after line 286: WriteResult (for write operations), TestResult (for individual tests), ValidationResult (for test suites). Each dataclass must have to_dict() method for JSON serialization. ValidationResult needs summary property and success property.
acceptanceCriteria: WriteResult dataclass defined with success, characteristic_name, error_code, error_message, duration_ms fields
TestResult dataclass defined with name, status, duration_ms, details, error fields
ValidationResult dataclass defined with device_address, device_name, tests fields
All dataclasses have to_dict() methods
ValidationResult.summary property calculates test statistics
ValidationResult.success property returns boolean
Dataclasses are importable from hubblenetwork.ready
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ready read-status command
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want to read just the Status characteristic so that I can verify version and provisioning flags during development. Implement read_status() function in ready.py with async variant _read_status_async(). Add ready read-status CLI command in cli.py. Returns StatusCharacteristic with version and flags (key_written, config_written, epoch_time_written). Supports --address (required), --timeout, --format options.
acceptanceCriteria: read_status() function in ready.py connects and reads Status characteristic
Function returns StatusCharacteristic dataclass
CLI command ready read-status requires --address parameter
JSON output matches spec format with version and flags objects
Tabular output displays version, mode, and flags
BleError raised and formatted correctly on connection failure
Exit code 0 on success, 2 on error
Duration tracking works correctly
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ready read-key-info command
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want to read the Device Key characteristic to determine encryption mode so that I can verify the device advertises the correct AES mode. Implement read_key_info() function in ready.py with async variant. Add ready read-key-info CLI command. Returns DeviceKeyInfo with encryption_mode (AES-256-CTR or AES-128-CTR) and key_size. Supports --address (required), --timeout, --format options.
acceptanceCriteria: read_key_info() function reads Device Key characteristic
Function returns DeviceKeyInfo dataclass
CLI command requires --address parameter
JSON output includes encryption_mode, encryption_mode_code, key_size_bytes
Tabular output displays encryption mode and key size
Correctly identifies AES-256-CTR (0x01) and AES-128-CTR (0x02)
BleError raised on connection failure
Exit code 0 on success, 2 on error
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ready read-config command
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want to read the Device Configuration characteristic so that I can verify EID type and pool size settings. Add DeviceConfig dataclass to ready.py with from_bytes() parser and to_display_string() method. Implement read_config() function with async variant. Add ready read-config CLI command. Parses eid_type (utc/counter), rotation_period, pool_size from 12-byte characteristic. Supports --address (required), --timeout, --format options.
acceptanceCriteria: DeviceConfig dataclass defined with eid_type, rotation_period, pool_size, raw_bytes
read_config() function reads Device Configuration characteristic
CLI command requires --address parameter
JSON output includes eid_type, eid_type_code, rotation_period_seconds, pool_size, raw_bytes
Correctly parses UTC mode (0x00) and Counter mode (0x01)
Tabular output displays configuration in human-readable format
Exit code 0 on success, 2 on error
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ready read-time command
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want to read the Epoch Time characteristic so that I can verify time synchronization. Implement read_time() function in ready.py that returns Unix timestamp as integer. Add ready read-time CLI command. Parses 8-byte little-endian timestamp. JSON output includes timestamp, iso8601 string, and skew (difference from current time). Supports --address (required), --timeout, --format options.
acceptanceCriteria: read_time() function reads Epoch Time characteristic
Function returns Unix timestamp as integer
CLI command requires --address parameter
JSON output includes timestamp, iso8601, skew_seconds
Tabular output displays timestamp, ISO date, and skew
Skew calculation shows positive (device ahead) or negative (device behind)
Exit code 0 on success, 2 on error
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ready write-key command
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want to write encryption keys to the Device Key characteristic so that I can test key provisioning in isolation. Implement write_key() function in ready.py that accepts 32-byte (AES-256) or 16-byte (AES-128) keys as hex strings. Add ready write-key CLI command with --key-hex parameter. Validates key length and format. Returns WriteResult. Supports --address (required), --key-hex (required), --timeout, --format options. Captures ATT error codes on write failure.
acceptanceCriteria: write_key() function writes Device Key characteristic
Function validates key is 32 or 16 bytes hex string
CLI command requires --address and --key-hex parameters
JSON output includes WriteResult with success status
ATT error codes captured and reported (0x0D for invalid length)
Exit code 0 on success, 1 on validation failure, 2 on error
Tabular output displays success or error message
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ready write-config command
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want to write configuration to the Device Configuration characteristic so that I can test config provisioning. Implement write_config() function in ready.py that accepts eid-type (utc/counter), rotation-period (must be 0), and pool-size (for counter mode). Add ready write-config CLI command. Validates: rotation_period == 0, pool_size > 0 for counter mode, pool_size == 0 for UTC mode. Returns WriteResult. Captures ATT error codes (0x84-0x87) on validation failures.
acceptanceCriteria: write_config() function writes Device Configuration characteristic
Function validates rotation_period is 0
Function validates pool_size constraints based on eid_type
CLI command requires --address and --eid-type parameters
CLI validates pool-size parameter based on eid-type
ATT error codes captured for invalid values (0x84, 0x85, 0x86, 0x87)
Exit code 0 on success, 1 on validation failure, 2 on error
JSON output includes WriteResult
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ready write-time command
userStories.description: As a firmware developer, I want to write epoch time to the Epoch Time characteristic so that I can test time provisioning. Implement write_time() function in ready.py that writes current Unix timestamp or accepts custom timestamp. Add ready write-time CLI command with optional --timestamp parameter (defaults to current time). Validates timestamp is reasonable (2020-2050 range). Returns WriteResult. Supports --address (required), --timestamp (optional), --timeout, --format options.
acceptanceCriteria: write_time() function writes Epoch Time characteristic
Function uses current time if no timestamp provided
Function validates timestamp is in reasonable range (2020-2050)
CLI command requires --address parameter
CLI accepts optional --timestamp parameter
Exit code 0 on success, 1 on validation failure, 2 on error
JSON output includes WriteResult with timestamp written
Tabular output displays success message with timestamp
Typecheck passes
